channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Metro Manila Trains to Suspend Operations for Holy Week Maintenance
item.description: Commuters in Metro Manila should take note of adjusted train schedules this coming Holy Week in 2026. Due to annual maintenance work, the LRT-1, LRT-2, and MRT-3 lines will temporarily suspend operati...

item.title: Japan clinch Women’s Asian Cup after defeating Matildas 1-0
item.description: Australia's hopes of lifting the Women’s Asian Cup were dashed after a narrow 1-0 loss to Japan in the final. The decisive moment came in the first half, when Maika Hamano scored the only goal of the ...

item.title: Adani Electricity Retains Top Position Among Indian Distribution Utilities for Second Year
item.description: The news article reports that Adani Electricity has been recognised as the leading electricity distribution utility in India for the second consecutive year. This ranking highlights the company’s cons...

item.title: Crystal: Pioneering Filipina Wrestler's Decade-Long Journey
item.description: Crystal stands as the pioneering Filipina female wrestler who blazed the trail for women in Philippine professional wrestling over the past decade. Starting in 2016 with the Philippine Wrestling Revol...

item.title: Tinubu’s Executive Order 9 Makes NNPC Pay Full PSC Profit Oil to Federation Account
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s Executive Order 9 don begin to show effect as the Federation Account don get full 100 percent profit oil wey come from Production Sharing Contracts (PSCs) for February 20...

item.title: Oketch Salah Hosts Jaoko Oburu and Omosh at His Elegant Home During Eid Celebrations
item.description: Oketch Salah, a prominent businessman and the late Raila Odinga's 'adopted' son, made a grand return to social media after some time away. His recent social media post showed him hosting family and fr...

item.title: University Academic Wins Defamation Case Over Comments on Indigenous Identity
item.description: A University of Regina academic, Michelle Coupal, has been awarded damages and legal fees after successfully suing researcher Darryl Leroux for defamation. Leroux had publicly accused Coupal of using ...

item.title: Mahlobo Emphasises Science-Based Solutions for South Africa's Water Crisis
item.description: South Africa’s Deputy Minister of Water and Sanitation, David Mahlobo, recently addressed the country’s ongoing water challenges at a Water Imbizo hosted by the University of South Africa (UNISA). He ...

item.title: Iran President urges India-led BRICS to intervene in halting escalating aggressions in the Middle East
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian held a telephonic conversation on March 21, 2026, where discussions centred around Iran’s ongoing conflicts and the role India can ...

item.title: A.P. Police Advises Public to Avoid Malicious APKs and Cyber Fraud Links
item.description: The Andhra Pradesh Police Headquarters has issued an advisory cautioning the public about the growing threat of APK-based frauds. Cyber criminals are increasingly using malicious apps disguised as PDF...

item.title: Karabo Khakhau withdraws from DA leadership race after tithe payment dispute
item.description: Democratic Alliance (DA) MP and national spokesperson Karabo Khakhau has withdrawn from competing for the party’s federal council deputy chair position after a dispute about a missed tithe payment of ...

item.title: Joburg council approves revised R90bn budget, facing revenue shortfalls and spending cuts
item.description: The City of Johannesburg has approved its adjusted budget of over R90bn for the 2025/2026 financial year after a week’s delay. The delay was due to time constraints in reviewing the budget. While the ...

item.title: Armed Boko Haram and ISWAP members hold Eid prayers in Borno after deadly Maiduguri bombings
item.description: Hundreds of Boko Haram and Islamic State West Africa Province (ISWAP) fighters were seen holding Eid prayers openly in the Lake Chad region of Borno State, only days after deadly bombings shook Maidug...

item.title: NNPP urges INEC to obey FCT High Court ruling recognizing Agbo Major-led leadership
item.description: The New Nigeria Peoples Party (NNPP) don call on the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) make dem follow the order wey come from FCT High Court wey Justice Bello Kawu give on March 17, 20...

item.title: India Increases Commercial LPG Allocation Amidst West Asia Conflict
item.description: The Indian government has decided to increase the allocation of commercial liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) to states and Union Territories by an additional 20%, bringing the total allocation to 50%. Thi...

item.title: Canada once printed gas rationing stamps during the 1979 oil crisis, economist notes modern parallels
item.description: During the 1979 oil crisis triggered by the Iranian Revolution, Canada’s federal government prepared to ration gasoline by printing official rationing stamps, each entitling the holder to 50 litres of...

item.title: Toronto sommelier launches approachable French wine brand, Praise Wines
item.description: The article tells the story of Scott Zebarth, a Toronto-based sommelier who co-founded Praise Wines with Mark Barr. Drawing on his experience managing wine programs at some of Toronto’s top restaurant...

item.title: Trump suggests Australia should help in Iran conflict, Albanese denies request
item.description: US President Donald Trump claimed that Australia had declined his request to participate in military operations in Iran, urging Canberra to 'get involved'. Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese d...

item.title: Court releases man due to insufficient evidence in mother’s death case
item.description: In Arusha, the High Court at Bukoba Sub-Registry has released Deus Boniphace after the prosecution failed to prove beyond reasonable doubt that he killed his 83-year-old mother, Ms Crisencia Boniphace...

item.title: Researchers uncover 48-dimensional topological structures in entangled light
item.description: Scientists from the University of the Witwatersrand in South Africa and Huzhou University in China have made a major breakthrough in quantum optics by uncovering hidden topological structures within e...
